- O node 10: pixels=10, boundary=[[40, 19], [40, 23], [41, 23], [41, 19]] - orange at rows 40-41, cols 19-23
- b node 11: pixels=15, boundary=[[42, 19], [42, 23], [44, 23], [44, 19]] - blue at rows 42-44, cols 19-23
- W node 7: pixels=3, boundary=[[31, 21], [32, 22], [32, 21]] - white cross at rows 31-32, cols 21-22
So the blocks are now at rows 40-44, cols 19-23. The UP action moved the blocks from rows 45-49 to rows 40-44 (UP 5 rows). The RIGHT action didn't change the column (still cols 19-23).
Wait, but I executed RIGHT first, then UP. The RIGHT action should have moved the blocks from cols 19-23 to cols 24-28. But the blocks are still at cols 19-23. So RIGHT didn't work from this position.
Actually, looking at the output, the blocks are at rows 40-44, cols 19-23. The UP action moved the blocks from rows 45-49 to rows 40-44. The RIGHT action didn't move the blocks horizontally.
So the blocks are at rows 40-44, cols 19-23. The white cross is at rows 31-32, cols 21-22. The blocks are below the cross, and the columns overlap (cols 19-23 includes cols 21-22).
If I move UP again, the blocks should move from rows 40-44 to rows 35-39. Then UP again to rows 30-34, which would overlap with the cross at rows 31-32.
Let me try UP twice to get to rows 30-34.

<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Blocks at rows 40-44, cols 19-23
# White cross at rows 31-32, cols 21-22
# UP moves blocks up 5 rows
# UP from 40-44 -> 35-39, then UP from 35-39 -> 30-34
# At rows 30-34, blocks overlap with cross at rows 31-32
action(['UP', 'UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
